advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Nutritional psychiatry identifies strong bidirectional links between diet quality and mental health outcomes. Omega-3 DHA/EPA, B vitamins, zinc, magnesium, and tryptophan precursors are directly implicated in neurotransmitter synthesis and mood regulation.
research_findings: Mediterranean-style dietary patterns are associated with a 25-35% lower risk of depression compared to Western dietary patterns. Gut microbiome health mediates the diet-brain connection through the gut-brain axis.
current_focus_areas: Building dietary protocols for depression, anxiety, and ADHD nutritional support; researching micronutrient supplementation for mental health optimization; coordinating with Gut-Brain Axis bot on neuronutrition protocols.
